ir_specs.statement: If there is no entry for the calling process, logname prints an error message and exits with a status of 1.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If there is no entry for the calling process, logname prints an error message and exits with a status of 1.

ir_specs.statement: users prints on a single line a blank-separated list of user names of users currently logged in to the current host.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: users prints on a single line a blank-separated list of user names of users currently logged in to the current host.

ir_specs.statement: Each user name corresponds to a login session, so if a user has more than one login session, that user’s name will appear the same number of times in the output.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Each user name corresponds to a login session, so if a user has more than one login session, that user’s name will appear the same number of times in the output.

ir_specs.statement: With no file argument, users extracts its information from a system-maintained file (often /var/run/utmp or /etc/utmp ).
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: With no file argument, users extracts its information from a system-maintained file (often /var/run/utmp or /etc/utmp ).

ir_specs.statement: If a file argument is given, users uses that file instead.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If a file argument is given, users uses that file instead.

ir_specs.statement: The users command is installed only on platforms with the POSIX <utmpx.h> include file or equivalent, so portable scripts should not rely on its existence on non-POSIX platforms.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The users command is installed only on platforms with the POSIX <utmpx.h> include file or equivalent, so portable scripts should not rely on its existence on non-POSIX platforms.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -b option Print the date and time of last system boot.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print the date and time of last system boot.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -d option Print information corresponding to dead processes.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print information corresponding to dead processes.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -H option Print a line of column headings.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print a line of column headings.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -l option List only the entries that correspond to processes via which the system is waiting for a user to login.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: List only the entries that correspond to processes via which the system is waiting for a user to login.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` --lookup option Attempt to canonicalize hostnames found in utmp through a DNS lookup.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Attempt to canonicalize hostnames found in utmp through a DNS lookup.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -p option List active processes spawned by init.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: List active processes spawned by init.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -q option Print only the login names and the number of users logged on.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print only the login names and the number of users logged on.

ir_specs.statement: The `who invocation` -r option Print the current (and maybe previous) run-level of the init process.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Print the current (and maybe previous) run-level of the init process.
